meta.title: Notification of changes in taxonomic opinion previously published outside the IJSEM
meta.journalTitle:
International Journal of Systematic and Evolutionary
 Microbiology
meta.section: List of Changes in Taxonomic Opinion no. 2

abstractHtml: The Bacteriological Code deals with the nomenclature of prokaryotes. This may include existing names (the Approved Lists of Bacterial Names) as well as new names and new combinations. In this sense the Code is also dealing indirectly with taxonomic opinions. However, as with most codes of nomenclature there are no mechanisms for formally recording taxonomic opinions that do not involve the creation of new names or new combinations. In particular, it would be desirable for taxonomic opinions resulting from the creation of synonyms or emended descriptions to be made widely available to the public. In 2004, the Editorial Board of the International Journal of Systematic and Evolutionary Microbiology (IJSEM) agreed unanimously that it was desirable to cover such changes in taxonomic opinions (i.e. the creation of synonyms or the emendation of circumscriptions) previously published outside the IJSEM, and to introduce a List of Changes in Taxonomic Opinion [Notification of changes in taxonomic opinion previously published outside the IJSEM; Euzéby et al. (2004). Int J Syst Evol Microbiol 54, 1429–1430]. Scientists wishing to have changes in taxonomic opinion included in future lists should send one copy of the pertinent reprint or a photocopy or a PDF file thereof to the IJSEM Editorial Office or to the Lists Editor. It must be stressed that the date of proposed taxonomic changes is the date of the original publication not the date of publication of the list. Taxonomic opinions included in the List of Changes in Taxonomic Opinion cannot be considered as validly published nor, in any other way, approved by the International Committee on Systematics of Prokaryotes and its Judicial Commission. The names that are to be used are those that are the ‘correct names' (in the sense of Principle 6) in the opinion of the bacteriologist, with a given circumscription, position and rank. A particular name, circumscription, position and rank does not have to be adopted in all circumstances. Consequently, the List of Changes in Taxonomic Opinion must be considered as a service to bacteriology and it has no ‘official character’, other than providing a centralized point for registering/indexing such changes in a way that makes them easily accessible to the scientific community.

    Jean Euzéby

summaryHtml:
This article announces a formal system for publishing changes in bacterial taxonomic opinions that were originally published outside the International Journal of Systematic and Evolutionary Microbiology (IJSEM). In 2004, the IJSEM Editorial Board established the List of Changes in Taxonomic Opinion to centralize and publicize taxonomic revisions, including the creation of synonyms and emended descriptions. The list serves as a registry to make such changes easily accessible to the scientific community. Importantly, the article clarifies that these changes are not formally validated by the International Committee on Systematics of Prokaryotes and do not constitute official approval. The dates of taxonomic changes are determined by their original publication, not by inclusion in the list. Scientists can submit relevant reprints or PDFs to the IJSEM Editorial Office for inclusion in future lists. The article provides the second list of such changes, documenting taxonomic revisions for various bacterial species and genera, including amendments to descriptions of organisms like Desulfitobacterium, Enterobacter, Halobacterium, and Thioalkalivibrio.

Key findings

  • IJSEM established a formal List of Changes in Taxonomic Opinion to centralize publication of bacterial taxonomic revisions previously published elsewhere
  • The list includes synonymies and emended descriptions of various prokaryotic taxa, making these changes easily accessible to the scientific community
  • Changes are dated according to their original publication, not their listing date
  • The list provides a centralized registry but has no official character and is not formally validated by the International Committee on Systematics of Prokaryotes
  • Scientists can submit relevant publications to IJSEM for inclusion in future lists of taxonomic changes
